reference.title: Exit codes reference
reference.shortdesc: markdown-validator exits with code 0 when all Required rules pass and code 1 when any Required rule fails. CI systems use these codes to gate pull request merges.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Reference for markdown-validator exit codes and their meaning in CI/CD pipelines.

row.entry: CI step succeeds. The pipeline continues. Suggested-rule failures do not affect this code.
One or more Required rules failed
CI step fails. The pipeline stops (unless configured to continue on error). The merge is blocked.

ul.li: Suggested-level failures do not change the exit code. A file with only Suggested failures exits with code 0.
A parse error (file has no front matter) produces exit code 1. The file is counted as a failed scan.
In a directory scan, one failing file sets the exit code to 1 for the entire run.

section.title: Checking the exit code in a shell script
section.codeblock: md-validate validate docs/ --rules rules/tutorial.json if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then echo "All required rules passed." else echo "Validation failed. Check the report for details." fi
